name: Pylon
blocker: Requirement for Admin-level access to generate API tokens makes it difficult to use as a self-serve tool without internal company permissions.
access_notes: The documentation explicitly states that only Admin users can create API tokens. There is no information regarding a self-serve developer or sandbox account that grants autonomous access.
api_notes: The documentation mentions using the API to fetch customer information, fetch issues, and create issues programmatically, representing core functional objects of the platform. The API reference is mentioned but not fully detailed in the provided sources.
mcp_notes: No mention of an official or community Model Context Protocol server in the documentation.
one_liner: Pylon is an AI-native B2B support platform that centralizes customer communications and automates issue resolution across multiple channels.

evidence.claim: Pylon's API allows programmatically fetching customer/issue info and creating issues, but requires Admin users to create API tokens.
Pylon is an AI-native B2B support platform for managing customer issues across multiple channels.

auth_notes: The documentation refers to the use of API tokens created by Admin users. No other authentication schemes are mentioned.
